programme.title: BBC News At Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 International SBS Midday
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: PBS Newshour
programme.desc: Reviews and analysis of the day's news, presented by Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett for the Public Broadcasting Service (PBS) in the United States.

programme.title: What Does Australia Really Think About
programme.desc: Casey Donovan investigates what Australia really thinks about obesity through emotional personal stories, confronting social experiments, covert filming and an in-depth nationwide survey.

programme.title: Seeds: Planting Hope Through Education
programme.desc: 'SEEDS: Planting Hope Through Education' is a grassroots documentary which transports you to Uganda and spotlights the crucial importance of education in eradicating poverty.

programme.title: The Cook Up With Adam Liaw
programme.desc: This one's for the crisp takers. Adam, hatted chef Will Houia and the Wolf of Wok Street Vincent Lim are all about their crispy business.

programme.title: Lost Treasures of Egypt
programme.desc: Archaeologists investigate the mysterious Pyramid Kings, and discover a lost temple.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed, and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: Letters And Numbers
programme.desc: Two contestants pit their linguistic and numerical skills against each other and the clock in this entertaining quiz show.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Tonight - Australian red wine, Swedish band Roxette and The Elder Scrolls and Critical Role: Exandria Unlimited. Contenders: Taryn Gorton, Megan Baehnisch, Paula Brougham, Mark Allen.

programme.title: SBS World News
programme.desc: Latest reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Bayeux To Argentan/ Lisieux To Giverny
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Bayeux to Argentan then on to Lisieux to Giverny.

programme.title: Insight
programme.desc: Is the rat race still worth running? Why are our attitudes towards work life balance changing?

programme.title: Dateline
programme.desc: Kumi Taguchi travels to France to meet the people saving its castles. With many properties cheaper than an average Aussie home, Dateline investigates the real cost of keeping them alive.

programme.title: SBS World News Late
programme.desc: Latest reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Living Black
programme.desc: Karla Grant travels to Western Australia to meet the rappers behind 'Banksia Beats', a hip-hop therapy program that's helping young detainees at Banksia Hill and Unit 18 find their voice through rap.

programme.title: Exit
programme.desc: The real world catches up with most people, and real problems concern the unreal as well. It's becoming clear that money can't buy everything... but maybe everyone.

programme.title: Breaking Point
programme.desc: Shots are fired in the Sunshine City shopping centre. It is pure coincidence that these people are here. In one of them, a long-fermenting insult leads to a violent outburst.

programme.title: Breaking Point
programme.desc: Four days before shots are fired in the Sunshine City shopping centre. There is a lot at stake in Mira's job today. But her boss, with whom she is having an affair, threatens to betray her.

programme.title: Breaking Point
programme.desc: Sarah, 27, sells her body in order to fund a lavish lifestyle. Her friends fear that she will end up in prison - or worse. Can Sarah get help before it's too late?

programme.title: Giving Back: Students Who Returned As Teachers
programme.desc: This heart-warming documentary follow the journey of former students in Dandenong North Primary School who came back to become teachers.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates, Jr. helps actors Isabella Rossellini, Anjelica Huston and Mia Farrow unearth revelations about their family histories, taking them beyond their famous cinematic forebears.

programme.title: NHK World English News Morning
programme.desc: English news from Japan's Broadcasting Corporation, NHK, delivering the latest in business, technology, health, environmental and world news.

programme.title: ANC Philippines The World Tonight
programme.desc: Multi-award winning newscast on ABS-CBN and ANC, The World Tonight delivers the top stories of the day, covering political, economic, and social sectors on both local and global fronts.

programme.title: DW English News
programme.desc: News and analysis of the top international and European news and current affairs from Berlin, in English.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News
programme.desc: News via satellite from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: BBC News At Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 English News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: DD India News Hour
programme.desc: The most recent political, sports, entertainment and socio-economic updates across India and the world from New Delhi, in English.

programme.title: Destination Flavour China Bitesize
programme.desc: All the best moments and bits from Destination Flavour China, led by famed chef and author Adam Liaw.

programme.title: Matched
programme.desc: Putting heartbreak behind her, Sonam begins the search for a new life partner. But when she comes up against the stigma that accompanies divorce, Sonam finds she must follow her own path.

programme.title: Hidden Beauty Of Spain
programme.desc: The journey begins in the Parque Nacionalde Donana. The Unesco World Heritage Site is located on the Costa de la Luz and is one of the most diverse wetlands in Europe.

programme.title: Van Gogh: The Real Story
programme.desc: In many ways, it is in Antwerp that Vincent begins the remarkable evolution that will ultimately make him one of the most important painters in modern art.

programme.title: BBC News At Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 International SBS Midday
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: PBS Newshour
programme.desc: Reviews and analysis of the day's news, presented by Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett for the Public Broadcasting Service (PBS) in the United States.

programme.title: Dateline
programme.desc: Kumi Taguchi travels to France to meet the people saving its castles. With many properties cheaper than an average Aussie home, Dateline investigates the real cost of keeping them alive.

programme.title: Insight
programme.desc: What happens when you're stuck between a rock and a hard place?

programme.title: Plat Du Tour
programme.desc: Loire Valley is known as the Garden of France. Guillaume is making a vegetarian version of the quiche Lorraine. He shares some of his mum's secret tips to create a dish to share with the whole family.

programme.title: Plat Du Tour
programme.desc: Tthe cuisine of Burgundy also has influences from Northern Europe and the Mediterranean, so what better way to celebrate than by making a rich red wine and beef stew with a surprise twist of orange.

programme.title: The Cook Up With Adam Liaw
programme.desc: Adam and his guests, chef Kay-Lete Tan and writer Benjamin Law work wonders with sweet citrus.

programme.title: Lost Treasures of Egypt
programme.desc: Archaeologists hunt for clues to the dramatic downfall of Egypt’s legendary last pharaoh – Cleopatra. They unravel the true story behind Cleopatra’s famous relationships with Julius Caesar and Marc Anthony. They discover a 2000-year old painting in the heart of the desert dating from her reign. And they hunt for her lost tomb in a secret tunnel beneath an ancient temple near Alexandria.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: An American television quiz show in which three contestants try to win money as they are quizzed on all types of trivia.

programme.title: Letters And Numbers
programme.desc: Two contestants pit their linguistic and numerical skills against each other and the clock in this entertaining quiz show.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Tonight, Aunty Donna's Web and TV series, Douglas MacArthur, Freaks and Geeks and the life of ballet dancer Nijinsky. They should keep you on your toes.

programme.title: SBS World News
programme.desc: Latest reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Chernobyl : Inside The Meltdown
programme.sub-title: Chernobyl: Chronology Of A Nuclear Disaster
programme.desc: The true, minute-by-minute account of the buildup to the meltdown.

programme.title: The Trial Of Michael Jackson
programme.desc: Using unseen footage, unheard audio tapes and exclusive interviews, this series uncovers the remarkable and troubling story behind the 2005 Michael Jackson trial.

programme.title: The Audacity
programme.desc: Inside the bubble of Silicon Valley, a data-mining CEO strives to turn insight and influence into profit and power in this darkly comedic look at the warped dreams, outsized egos, and ethical lapses of the self-styled inventors of the future.

programme.title: SBS World News Late
programme.desc: Latest reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Rise Of The Raven
programme.desc: Pope Eugenius declares a Holy Crusade against the Ottoman Empire and entrusts his papal envoy, cardinal Cesarini with the task of recruiting Hunyadi.

programme.title: Blue Lights
programme.desc: Grace Ellis investigates the punishment shooting of a young McIntyre drug runner, and her old skills as a social worker help get the evidence she needs to bring charges against the gang. However, she is stalled by a senior colleague, who makes it clear that she should back off. When Gordy pulls away from Angela, she becomes desperate for help.

programme.title: Blue Lights
programme.desc: The police ombudsman investigates the team after a disastrous night shift.

programme.title: Classified
programme.desc: In the remote region of Ghanim in the Maghreb, two Quebec engineers are taken hostage; Rachel supervises, on orders from Ottawa, an unsuccessful attempt to capture Robin Denard.

programme.title: Classified
programme.desc: The hunt for Denard gains momentum, but the latter thwarts it and taunts the SSC; Thomassin, who is investigating the hostage taking, consults Philip Miller, who has fallen into disgrace.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots
programme.sub-title: Off The Farm: Melissa McCarthy, Eric Stonestreet
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates, Jr. explores the humble roots of comedic actors Melissa McCarthy and Eric Stonestreet, retracing the ancestral journeys that laid the groundwork for their success.

programme.title: Destination Flavour China Bitesize
programme.sub-title: Steamed Oysters With Garlic, Ginger And Crab Tomalley
programme.desc: Tomalley is the name given to the digestive organs of shellfish like crabs and lobsters. Sometimes going by the names crab fat, lobster pâté, or mustard it is a delicacy. It has a colour that can vary from grey-green to yellow and bright orange when cooked You can remove it from the body of crabs that have been steamed or otherwise cooked.

programme.title: Destination Flavour China Bitesize
programme.desc: This precursor to the now-famous Hainanese chicken rice relies on having an exceptionally good quality chicken. On Hainan, chickens from Wenchang are the most prized. They’re fed on coconut, peanut bran, corn and sweet potato and their flavourful flesh and fat, and delicate skin make them one of Hainan’s four famous delicacies.

programme.title: NHK World English News Morning
programme.desc: English news from Japan's Broadcasting Corporation, NHK, delivering the latest in business, technology, health, environmental and world news.

programme.desc: Multi-award winning newscast on ABS-CBN and ANC, The World Tonight delivers the top stories of the day, covering political, economic, and social sectors on both local and global fronts.

programme.title: Destination Flavour China Bitesize
programme.desc: All the best moments and bits from Destination Flavour China, led by famed chef and author Adam Liaw.

programme.title: Matched
programme.desc: A mother's wish surprises everyone when she proposes her daughter Harpreet have an arranged marriage to her flat mate Sahil. Unexpectedly, Sahil agrees to sacrifice his dream of a Hindu wedding.

programme.title: Hidden Beauty Of Spain
programme.desc: The route leads from San Sebastian in the Basque Country to the unique wooden terraces of the Anana salt flats. Particularly pure salt has been harvested here for thousands of years.

programme.title: Van Gogh: The Real Story
programme.desc: Perhaps the most well-known fact about Vincent's troubled life is that he cuts his ear. Dive into the horrifying story of what it is that inspires him to do something so drastic.

programme.title: BBC News At Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 International SBS Midday
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: PBS Newshour
programme.desc: Reviews and analysis of the day's news, presented by Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett for the Public Broadcasting Service (PBS) in the United States.
